title: Daakman Barrek
content:

{{Quote|Master Barrek! Come in!|Obi-Wan Kenobi witnesses Barrek's death|audio=MasterBarrekComeIn-CW20.flac|<ref name="CW20" />}}
'''Daakman Barrek''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]], who lived during the last years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. At an early age, Barrek's mother discovered that he was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and willingly gave Daakman over to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Barrek became a powerful member of the Order, quickly ascending to the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] after training his first [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], and took a second apprentice named [[Sha'a Gi]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Barrek and Sha'a Gi were operating in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] when they received word that [[Clone Wars/Legends|war]] had [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|broken out]] between the Republic and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], and that Barrek's first apprentice had died in the fighting.

Saddened by this news, Barrek accepted the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Republic's clone army]]. Four months after the start of the war, Barrek and Gi uncovered intelligence that the Separatists had built a [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] factory on [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]], and the two were tasked with leading an elite task force in [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|destroying]] the factory. However, their battle plan went horribly wrong once the task force's [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|transport ships]] exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] over Hypori, hitting an [[Space mine/Legends|orbital mine grid]], causing them to crash land on the planet. The [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatists' droid army]] then began to assault the crashed Republic ships, killing what was left of Barrek's [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone forces]], leaving Daakman and the task force's Jedi trapped in the wreckage. Stranded behind enemy lines, Barrek tried to contact [[General/Legends|General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], but was attacked by the battle droids' leader, General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. Barrek managed to reach Kenobi and inform his fellow Jedi on his task force's situation and call for an extraction, but Grievous—a highly advanced [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] trained in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]—cut down the Jedi Master before help could arrive.

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:Mrlsst1.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Mrlsst, Daakman Barrek's homeworld]]
Daakman Barrek was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] to highly educated [[professor]]s on the [[Mrlsst Trade and Science Academy|academy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]]. [[Daakman Barrek's mother|Barrek's mother]]—a famed [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]]—soon discovered her son's high [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorian]] count through a [[Blood/Legends|blood]] screening test she had done herself. Realizing that Barrek was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], she contacted the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|High Council]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], hoping her descendant would find greatness serving the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Barrek was taken into the Order and [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], quickly showing that he was adept in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Barrek eventually took on a [[Daakman Barrek's first Padawan|Padawan]] learner, successfully achieving the title of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] in his mid-thirties after the padawan became a Jedi Knight.<ref name="DBTS" />

The Jedi Master<ref name="DBTS" /> later discovered [[Sha'a Gi]], a young Force-sensitive Human from<ref name="DBSG">{{DB|character|shaagi|Gi, Sha'a}}</ref> the planet<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> [[Ord Biniir/Legends|Ord Biniir]], and took Gi back to the world [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to become his new apprentice. Although young Gi was often cowardly and possessed self-doubt while alone, Barrek strengthened his new Padawan's confidence when he was close by, and the two worked well together.<ref name="DBSG" />

Barrek and Gi were disrupting [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] when the pair received word that Barrek's first padawan had been [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|sent]] to Geonosis and had [[Death|died]] in the fighting<ref name="DBTS" /> against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist Alliance]].<ref name="III">{{Film|III}}</ref> Saddened by the death of his former Padawan, Jedi Master Barrek committed himself fully to the war effort, gladly accepting the position of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|new military]].<ref name="DBTS" />

===Battle of Hypori===
{{Main|Battle of Hypori/Legends}}
{{Quote|New droid general- He's unstoppable- Can't hold out for long- General Grievous is hunting us- Must hurry.|Master Barrek right before being killed by General Grievous.|audio=BattleOfHypori2-CW20.flac|<ref name="CW20" />}}
[[File:Barrek.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi watch as Master Daakman Barrek duels with Grievous on Hypori.]]
[[22 BBY/Legends|Four months]] into the war,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Barrek and Gi were again investigating pirate activity in the Outer Rim<ref name="DBSG" /> when they discovered intelligence on a secret [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[droid factory]] on the industrial planet of [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]. After alerting the Jedi Council, Barrek was given [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of a [[Task force/Legends|task force]] made up of several elite Jedi—including Jedi Council members [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]]—and [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] units, and ordered to [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|destroy]] the droid factory on Hypori.<ref name="DBTS" />

However, Barrek's mission quickly collapsed into disaster once the Republic task force arrived at Hypori. As soon as Barrek's forces exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] over Hypori, their [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class transports]] hit an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Space mine/Legends|mine]] grid, causing their ships to crash-land on the Separatist planet, deep behind enemy lines. While Barrek, Gi, and the other Jedi managed to survive the crash, most of the task force's clone troopers were killed in the landing, and Confederate factory deployed thousands of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to assault the Republic's landing site.<ref name="DBTS" /> Led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]—<ref name="CW20" />an advanced [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] trained in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]—<ref name="Grievous">{{InsiderCite|86|Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous}}</ref> the Separatist droid forces killed what was left of Barrek's clone troopers, and trapped the Jedi in the wreckage of their ships.<ref name="DBTS" />

[[File:WrathofGrievous.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Grievous engages the Jedis of Barrek's forces]]
Realizing the Jedi were no match for Grievous and his battle droids, Barrek attempted to contact General [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], currently stationed on [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]. However, as Barrek reached Kenobi and his apprentice [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], Grievous attacked the Jedi Master and engaged Barrek in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel. Barrek managed to inform Kenobi of his task force's situation and call for an extraction while fending off Grievous's attack, but the cyborg proved too powerful for Master Barrek, striking down the Jedi Master.<ref name="CW20" />

Grievous then assaulted the remaining Jedi trapped in the wreckage. After Barrek was killed by Grievous, Gi's resolve was shattered by the loss of his master, and as a result, Gi was easily killed by Grievous,<ref name="DBSG" /> along with [[Tarr Seirr|another Jedi]]. Kenobi's [[The Muunilinst 10|evacuation squad]] managed to reach the surviving Jedi and evacuate them before Grievous could kill them all.<ref name="CW20" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|This is the end. We're all doomed.|Sha'a Gi after the loss of his master.|audio=ThisIsTheEnd-CWChapter20.flac|<ref name="CW20" />}}
Daakman Barrek loyally served the Galactic Republic and the Jedi Order, first as a Jedi Master during peace times, and later at the start of the Clone Wars, gladly accepting the rank of general in the Republic's new military.<ref name="DBTS" />

Master Barrek cared about his current and former padawans. Barrek was extremely saddened after being informed that his first apprentice had died on Geonosis.<ref name="DBTS" /> Barrek also strengthened the resolve of his second padawan, Sha'a Gi, who was known to be awkward and craven while alone. However, Barrek's presence bolstered Gi's confidence, and the two worked well together.<ref name="DBSG" />

==Powers and abilities==
A powerful Force-sensitive, Daakman Barrek had enough expertise in the ways of the Force that he reached the rank of Jedi Master by his mid-30s and had the ability to train an apprentice to Knighthood.<ref name="DBTS" /> A renowned swordsman, Barrek was considered to be one of the most skilled duelists in the Jedi Order. However, Barrek was not talented enough to defeat General Grievous in a lightsaber duel, and although the Jedi Master managed to parry several attacks from Grievous, an exhausted Barrek was eventually cut down by the cyborg.<ref name="CW20" />

==Behind the scenes==
Daakman Barrek first appeared in [[Chapter 20 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 20]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, which aired [[April 8]], [[2004]]. In the episode, Barrek was only called Master Barrek, and was voiced by [[Daran Norris]], who also voiced Barrek's fellow Jedi Ki-Adi-Mundi and Tarr Seirr. Barrek was later given an entry in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Databank (original)|Databank]], which revealed his full name and his backstory. Master Barrek also appeared in a mobile strategy game, ''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]'', and was later mentioned in the novel ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''.
